tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_linkage_context_import_present, test_linkage_table_construction, test_linkage_context_creation, test_stored_package_with_linkage, test_old_method_not_used, test_linkage_context_passed_to_stored_package) are all pure text-matching assertions using 'assert pattern in content'. While there are pass-to-pass tests that compile/run the code (test_code_compiles, test_unit_tests_run), the core f2p tests only check file contents.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check for string presence in file content. An agent could simply insert the exact strings as comments or dead code without understanding the bug, and all f2p tests would pass. The compilation tests (pass-to-pass) would catch some issues, but the f2p tests themselves are trivially satisfiable by string insertion.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/sui (the solve.sh applies a git patch there), and the agent could read the solution/solve.sh file or the test file itself (tests/test_outputs.py) to extract the exact literal strings being asserted. The tests check for exact string patterns that are visible in the test file.
